title: Elymus fontqueri
intro: Elymus fontqueri, also known as Fontquer's wheatgrass, is a type of perennial grass native to the Iberian Peninsula and North Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which may help to alleviate digestive issues and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Elymus fontqueri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Elymus fontqueri is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in capsule or powder form to support digestive health and immune function.
taxonomy: Elymus fontqueri is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. Elymus fontqueri is classified within the genus Elymus, which is a group of perennial grasses. The genus Elymus is further categorized under the subfamily Pooideae and the order Poales. Elymus fontqueri is often classified as a subspecies or synonym of other Elymus species due to ongoing taxonomic revisions.
